sourceMovie.title: Bad Sister
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Bad Sister for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a tv movie rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Doug Campbell keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The thriller and tv movie blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Infidelity in Suburbia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Bad Sister clicked for you, Infidelity in Suburbia (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in thriller and tv movie, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Bad Sister?
faq.answer: Try Infidelity in Suburbia, Basic Instinct, Wild Things—each shares DNA with Bad Sister while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Bad Sister?
faq.answer: Bad Sister (2015) is best described as Thriller, TV Movie. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Bad Sister worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
